 {"id":"bd-2ay73","title":"Add process name fallback for agent detection","description":"## Summary
When pane title doesn't match known agent patterns, fall back to querying the foreground process name to detect agents.

## Context
- GitHub Issue #20 reports agent detection fails when pane title changes dynamically
- Shell prompts and tmux hooks can change pane titles
- Need fallback detection mechanism

## Requirements
1. Primary: Check pane title against agent patterns (existing behavior)
2. Fallback: Query foreground process name via tmux
3. Cache results to avoid repeated lookups
4. Support all known agents: claude, codex, gemini-cli, aider, cursor

## Technical Approach
tmux list-panes -F '#{pane_current_command}' or tmux display-message -p -t PANE '#{pane_current_command}'

Process name patterns to detect:
- claude, claude-code -> Claude Code
- codex -> Codex CLI  
- gemini -> Gemini CLI
- aider -> Aider
- cursor -> Cursor

## Files to Modify
- internal/agent/patterns.go - Add process name patterns
- internal/robot/probe.go - Add fallback detection logic

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Agent detected even when pane title is custom/dynamic
- [ ] No performance regression (cache process lookups)
- [ ] Existing title-based detection still works as primary
- [ ] Tests cover both title and process detection paths","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T05:02:29.215906059Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T05:07:30.081155970Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T05:07:30.081113260Z","close_reason":"Implemented process name fallback in internal/tmux/session.go","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-2b0eb","title":"AccountRotator: Track account rotation history","description":"## Purpose
Track account rotation history per pane to enable analytics, debugging, and rate limit learning across sessions.

## Implementation Details

```go
// RotationHistory tracks all account rotations with persistence
type RotationHistory struct {
    dataDir   string
    history   map[string][]RotationRecord // pane -> records
    mu        sync.RWMutex
    logger    *slog.Logger
}

type RotationRecord struct {
    Timestamp       time.Time `json:"timestamp"`
    SessionPane     string    `json:"session_pane"`
    AgentType       string    `json:"agent_type"`
    Project         string    `json:"project"`
    PreviousAccount string    `json:"previous_account"`
    NewAccount      string    `json:"new_account"`
    TriggerPattern  string    `json:"trigger_pattern"`
    TimeSinceLast   time.Duration `json:"time_since_last,omitempty"`
}

// RecordRotation logs a rotation event
func (h *RotationHistory) RecordRotation(r RotationRecord) {
    h.mu.Lock()
    defer h.mu.Unlock()
    
    paneHistory := h.history[r.SessionPane]
    
    // Calculate time since last rotation for this pane
    if len(paneHistory) > 0 {
        last := paneHistory[len(paneHistory)-1]
        r.TimeSinceLast = r.Timestamp.Sub(last.Timestamp)
    }
    
    h.history[r.SessionPane] = append(paneHistory, r)
    
    h.logger.Info("rotation recorded",
        "session_pane", r.SessionPane,
        "agent_type", r.AgentType,
        "previous", r.PreviousAccount,
        "new", r.NewAccount,
        "time_since_last", r.TimeSinceLast,
        "total_rotations", len(h.history[r.SessionPane]))
}

// GetRotationStats returns statistics for analysis
func (h *RotationHistory) GetRotationStats(agentType string) RotationStats {
    h.mu.RLock()
    defer h.mu.RUnlock()
    
    var stats RotationStats
    for _, records := range h.history {
        for _, r := range records {
            if r.AgentType == agentType {
                stats.TotalRotations++
                stats.AvgTimeBetween += r.TimeSinceLast
                stats.AccountUsage[r.NewAccount]++
            }
        }
    }
    // ... calculate averages
    return stats
}

// SaveToFile persists history to .ntm/rotation_history.json
func (h *RotationHistory) SaveToFile() error {
    // Similar to ratelimit.RateLimitTracker.SaveToDir pattern
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log each rotation with full context
- Log time since last rotation for pattern analysis
- Log statistics on request (debug level)

## Integration with Existing Code
- Follow persistence pattern from internal/ratelimit/tracker.go
- Store in .ntm directory alongside rate_limits.json

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Track rotation records with timestamps
- [ ] Calculate time between rotations per pane
- [ ] Provide aggregate statistics per agent type
- [ ] Persist to disk in .ntm directory
- [ ] Load history on startup
- [ ] Thread-safe access with RWMutex
- [ ] Unit tests for stats calculations","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:01:53.149378960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T16:49:31.126986810Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2b0eb","depends_on_id":"bd-3d4i9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2b0eb","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2b35","title":"EPIC: Session Profiles & Templates System","description":"**IDEA #12 - REDUCES DECISION FATIGUE (73% confidence)**

## What It Does
Pre-configured session layouts for common development patterns. One command to spawn a session optimized for code review, refactoring, greenfield development, etc.

## Why This Matters
- Currently: Users must decide agent counts and roles each time
- After: Common patterns are one command away
- Especially valuable for newcomers learning optimal configurations

## Built-in Templates
ntm spawn myproject --template=code-review
ntm spawn myproject --template=refactor
ntm spawn myproject --template=greenfield
ntm spawn myproject --template=bug-hunt
ntm spawn myproject --template=documentation

## Template Definition Format (templates.toml)
[[templates]]
name = 'code-review'
description = 'Optimized for reviewing PRs and providing feedback'
agents = { cc = 2, cod = 1 }
personas = ['reviewer', 'implementer', 'tester']
initial_prompts = [
    { target = 'cc', message = 'You are a code reviewer...' },
    { target = 'cod', message = 'You will implement fixes...' }
]

## Success Criteria

1. Built-in templates for 5 common workflows
2. Custom template definition via TOML files
3. ntm spawn --template NAME works correctly
4. ntm templates list shows available templates
5. Templates include agent configs and prompts
6. Templates stored in config directory
7. Unit tests for template parsing (80% coverage)
8. E2E tests verify template-based spawning","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-kb0u","title":"Error Handling Tests: CM unavailability and fallbacks","description":"## Error Handling Tests for CM Integration

### Error Scenarios
1. **CM not installed**
   - which cm returns nothing
   - Verify graceful degradation
   - Verify warning message

2. **CM subprocess crash**
   - CM process exits mid-operation
   - Verify reconnection attempt
   - Verify cached context used

3. **CASS unavailable**
   - Session search fails
   - Verify CM-only mode works

4. **Memory corruption**
   - Invalid JSON from CM
   - Malformed guard data
   - Verify safe defaults used

### Fallback Verification
- Test spawn works without CM (just warns)
- Test spawn works with partial CM (recalls but no guards)
- Test context degradation is logged clearly

### Logging
log.Printf("[CM-ERROR] %s: %v", operation, err)
log.Printf("[CM-FALLBACK] Using cached context: %d bytes", len(cached))

 {"id":"bd-khux0","title":"EPIC: Context Continuity System - Compound Dont Compact","description":"## Philosophy: "Compound, Don't Compact"

This EPIC implements a context management system inspired by [Continuous-Claude-v3](https://github.com/parcadei/Continuous-Claude-v3). The core insight: **each compaction is lossy compression**—after several compactions, agents work with degraded context. Instead of fighting compaction, we:

1. **Extract state before compaction** - Pre-compact hook captures context
2. **Clear and reload fresh** - Start new session with full signal from handoff
3. **Use token-efficient YAML** - ~400 tokens vs ~2000 markdown
4. **Show status in real-time** - Dashboard displays goal/now from latest handoff

## Why This Approach Is Superior

**Current approach problems:**
- Agents suddenly hit context limits, lose mid-work context
- Each compaction degrades signal quality
- No structured way to preserve decisions, learnings, next steps
- Users never know "where did we leave off?"

**CC-v3 approach benefits:**
- Handoffs preserve 100% of key information
- Fresh context = full signal, no degradation
- Structured format ensures nothing is lost
- Status line shows current goal/now at a glance
- Works with multiple concurrent sessions

## YAML Handoff Format (~400 tokens)

```yaml
---
session: {session-name}
date: YYYY-MM-DD
status: complete|partial|blocked
outcome: SUCCEEDED|PARTIAL_PLUS|PARTIAL_MINUS|FAILED
---

goal: {What this session accomplished - shown in statusline}
now: {What next session should do first - shown in statusline}
test: {Command to verify this work}

done_this_session:
  - task: {First completed task}
    files: [{file1.go}, {file2.go}]

blockers: [{any blocking issues}]
questions: [{unresolved questions for next session}]

decisions:
  - {decision_name}: {rationale}

findings:
  - {key_finding}: {details}

worked: [{approaches that worked}]
failed: [{approaches that failed and why}]

next:
  - {First next step}
  - {Second next step}

files:
  created: [{new files}]
  modified: [{changed files}]
```

## Key Fields

- **goal + now**: REQUIRED, shown in status line/dashboard
- **done_this_session**: What was accomplished with file references
- **decisions**: Important choices and rationale for "future self"
- **findings**: Key learnings discovered
- **worked/failed**: What to repeat vs avoid
- **next**: Action items for resuming

## Directory Structure

```
.ntm/
├── handoffs/
│   ├── {session-name}/
│   │   ├── 2026-01-19_14-30_auth-complete.yaml
│   │   └── auto-handoff-{timestamp}.yaml
│   └── general/
└── ledgers/
    └── CONTINUITY_{session}.md
```

## Lifecycle Hooks Integration

1. **Pre-Compact Hook**: When agent approaches context limit (70%+) or manual /clear
   - Parse recent output/transcript
   - Generate YAML handoff automatically
   - Save to handoffs directory

2. **Session-Start Hook**: When spawning new session
   - Find most recent handoff for this session
   - Extract goal/now fields
   - Inject into initial agent context

3. **Session-End Hook**: On kill/exit
   - Prompt for handoff creation if work in progress

## CLI Commands

- `ntm handoff [session]` - Create manual handoff for session
- `ntm resume [session|path]` - Resume from handoff
- `ntm context status` - Show context and handoff status

## Integration Points

- **Dashboard**: Show goal/now/context% in top status bar
- **BV**: Include active bead IDs in handoffs
- **Agent Mail**: Thread IDs for related conversations
- **CM**: Query relevant memories on resume

## Success Criteria

- Agents can be cleared and resumed with zero context loss
- Status line shows current goal/now from latest handoff
- Automatic handoffs triggered before context exhaustion
- YAML format uses ~400 tokens (95% reduction from raw)
- Works with multiple concurrent sessions

## Relationship to Existing Beads

This EPIC subsumes and improves upon:
- bd-ljrn (Proactive Context Window Management)
- bd-2i44 (Structured Handoff Protocol)
- bd-3dxk (End-of-Session Summarization)

Complements:
- bd-38kx (Smart Session Recovery) - provides handoff data
- bd-2n9s (Cross-Session Memory via CM) - procedural memory","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

-{"id":"bd-kjihq","title":"Swarm CLI: Add --output flag for plan JSON export","description":"## Purpose
Add `--output` flag to the `ntm swarm` subcommand for writing the generated plan to a JSON file.

**NOTE**: This is a SUBCOMMAND flag for `ntm swarm`, NOT a top-level robot flag.

## Command Interface

```bash
# Subcommand usage (NOT robot mode)
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/projects --output=plan.json
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/projects --dry-run --output=plan.json

# Robot mode for querying swarm state is separate (see bd-3cdsi)
ntm --robot-snapshot  # Includes swarm state in unified output
```

## Implementation Details

```go
// In cmd/ntm/swarm.go - subcommand flag, not root robot flag
var outputPath string

func init() {
    swarmCmd.Flags().StringVar(&outputPath, "output", "",
        "Write swarm plan to JSON file (optional)")
}

func runSwarm(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    // ... generate plan ...
    
    if outputPath != "" {
        if err := writePlanToFile(plan, outputPath); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("write plan: %w", err)
        }
        slog.Info("plan written", "path", outputPath)
    }
    return nil
}

func writePlanToFile(plan *swarm.SwarmPlan, path string) error {
    dir := filepath.Dir(path)
    if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("create directory: %w", err)
    }
    
    data, err := json.MarshalIndent(plan, "", "  ")
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("marshal plan: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Atomic write
    tmpPath := path + ".tmp"
    if err := os.WriteFile(tmpPath, data, 0644); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    return os.Rename(tmpPath, path)
}
```

## Output Format
```json
{
  "created_at": "2026-01-22T10:30:00Z",
  "scan_dir": "/home/user/projects",
  "allocations": [...],
  "total_cc": 10,
  "total_cod": 8,
  "total_gmi": 6,
  "sessions": [...]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Flag is on swarm subcommand (not root)
- [ ] Works with --dry-run
- [ ] Atomic write via temp file
- [ ] Parent directory auto-creation
- [ ] Clear error messages","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:02:27.275998902Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T03:17:54.563768689Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kjihq","depends_on_id":"bd-2pgo6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:30Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-kjihq","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:30Z","created_by":"import"}]}

## Command Interface

```bash
# Subcommand usage (NOT robot mode)
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/projects --output=plan.json
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/projects --dry-run --output=plan.json

# Robot mode for querying swarm state is separate (see bd-3cdsi)
ntm --robot-snapshot  # Includes swarm state in unified output
```

## Implementation Details

```go
// In cmd/ntm/swarm.go - subcommand flag, not root robot flag
var outputPath string

func init() {
    swarmCmd.Flags().StringVar(&outputPath, "output", "",
        "Write swarm plan to JSON file (optional)")
}

func runSwarm(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    // ... generate plan ...
    
    if outputPath != "" {
        if err := writePlanToFile(plan, outputPath); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("write plan: %w", err)
        }
        slog.Info("plan written", "path", outputPath)
    }
    return nil
}

func writePlanToFile(plan *swarm.SwarmPlan, path string) error {
    dir := filepath.Dir(path)
    if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0755); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("create directory: %w", err)
    }
    
    data, err := json.MarshalIndent(plan, "", "  ")
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("marshal plan: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Atomic write
    tmpPath := path + ".tmp"
    if err := os.WriteFile(tmpPath, data, 0644); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    return os.Rename(tmpPath, path)
}
```

## Output Format
```json
{
  "created_at": "2026-01-22T10:30:00Z",
  "scan_dir": "/home/user/projects",
  "allocations": [...],
  "total_cc": 10,
  "total_cod": 8,
  "total_gmi": 6,
  "sessions": [...]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Flag is on swarm subcommand (not root)
- [ ] Works with --dry-run
- [ ] Atomic write via temp file
- [ ] Parent directory auto-creation

 {"id":"bd-kk81t","title":"Implement recommendation generator","description":"Implement recommendation generator for diagnosed issues.

## Recommendations per Status

### rate_limited
- action: wait_or_caam
- reason: 'Output contains rate limit pattern'
- Parse reset_time if available
- auto_fixable: false (requires human decision)

### unresponsive
- action: kill_restart
- reason: 'No output for N seconds, process state: X'
- auto_fixable: true
- fix_command: 'ntm --robot-restart-pane <session> <pane>'

### crashed
- action: restart_cc
- reason: 'No agent process' or 'Zombie process'
- auto_fixable: true
- fix_command: 'ntm --robot-restart-pane <session> <pane>'

## Output Format

Each recommendation includes:
- pane: int
- status: string
- action: string
- reason: string (human readable)
- auto_fixable: bool
- fix_command: string (if auto_fixable)
- Additional context (reset_time, etc.)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:27:33.219751841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.657219571Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.657174857Z","close_reason":"Implemented as part of bd-31e1f diagnose.go - includes --diagnose-pane flag, overall health classification, --diagnose-fix auto-remediation, recommendation generator, and health classification engine","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kk81t","depends_on_id":"bd-31e1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:30Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-krqz","title":"Add ntm health --auto-restart-stuck - detect and restart agents that appear stuck (no output for N minutes)","description":"Add ntm health --auto-restart-stuck - detect and restart agents that appear stuck (no output for N minutes).

## Problem Being Solved

Agents sometimes hang or get stuck. Currently requires manual detection and restart.

## Command Design

```bash
ntm health SESSION --auto-restart-stuck [--threshold DURATION] [--dry-run]
```

### Behavior
1. Monitors all agent panes for output activity
2. Detects panes with no output for threshold duration
3. Automatically restarts stuck panes

### Detection Logic
- Track last output timestamp per pane
- Pane is 'stuck' if no output for threshold (default: 5m)
- Ignore panes that are intentionally idle (awaiting user input)

### Options
- --threshold: Duration before considering stuck (default: 5m)
- --dry-run: Report stuck panes without restarting

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-health-restart-stuck=SESSION [--threshold=DURATION]

Returns JSON:
```json
{
  "session": "my_session",
  "stuck_panes": [3, 5],
  "restarted": [3, 5],
  "threshold": "5m"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. --auto-restart-stuck flag added to ntm health command
2. Detects panes with no output for threshold duration
3. Default threshold is 5 minutes
4. --threshold flag allows custom duration
5. --dry-run reports without restarting
6. Restart uses existing --robot-restart-pane logic
7. Robot mode returns JSON with stuck/restarted panes

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/cmd/health_test.go
- Test stuck detection logic
- Test threshold parsing

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-AUTO-RESTART] Auto-restart Stuck Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-AUTO-RESTART]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing --auto-restart-stuck"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. ntm health --auto-restart-stuck detects stuck agents
2. No output for N minutes triggers detection
3. Configurable threshold (default 5 minutes)

6. Unit tests for detection logic
7. E2E test with simulated stuck agent","notes":"## Testing Requirements

- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
